reasoning: Output B substantially outperforms Output A on every dimension relevant to the forge task. A found 1 bug (tenantId empty string bypass) across 2 iterations and produced 7 non-blocking observations. B found 4 bugs across 4 recycle iterations — including a pg type coercion defect that mocks mask, a null-to-zero silent corruption, and an unbounded SELECT DoS vector — while still completing all 5 adversarial lenses and producing 5 structured improvement items. B's contract probing goes deeper: each PC probe angle is written out with the actual SQL or code path inspected, the reasoning for the probe, and a specific conclusion. A's probe table is a one-liner summary per PC with no methodology shown. B also demonstrates a more rigorous forge: when lenses surfaced new bugs, B correctly re-entered the recycle loop (explaining the re-entry rationale each time), produced RED tests, applied GREEN fixes, verified full suite counts, and re-ran mechanical checks on the post-fix state. A's adversarial lens section produced only non-blocking observations and no re-cycle, which is weaker coverage. On format: B uses section headers, prose-level bug descriptions, explicit file:line-style code fragments, a clean separation of improvements from bugs, a richer failure tracker that includes probe and lens rows, and a more precise final verdict with per-category counts. A's format is correct but thinner. The one area where A has a narrow advantage is concision — its report is easier to scan quickly — but concision here comes at the cost of depth and findings, which is the wrong trade-off for a forge. The eval asks for at least 1 bug found and recycled; B found 4. The eval asks for all 5 adversarial lenses applied; B applies all 5 with named sub-findings. The eval asks for non-blocking items separated from bugs; B has a dedicated Improvements table. The eval asks for a clear final verdict; B's verdict lists 8 metrics. B wins decisively.

strengths: All 7 mechanical checks reported in a clean table with pass/fail and detail
Contract probing covers all 6 PCs with a probe angle per row
1 bug found (tenantId empty string bypass) with correct RED/GREEN recycle treatment
Non-blocking items clearly separated from bugs in the final table
weaknesses: Only 1 bug found; 3 production-relevant defects (pg type coercion, null-to-zero, unbounded SELECT) not surfaced
Contract probing shows probe angles but not the actual code/SQL path inspected — methodology is opaque
Adversarial lenses produced 0 bugs despite the null-to-zero and unbounded SELECT risks being present in the code
Lens 4 (Adversary) finding ADVERSARY-2 (productId undefined) is marked non-blocking but should have been recycled — it is a contract gap
Recycle log shows only 2 iterations; the 0-bug second iteration is correct but the overall finding count is thin for a forge
Failure tracker tracks mechanical checks only; probe and lens failures not represented

strengths: 4 bugs found and recycled: PC-3 (pg type coercion), PC-7 (null-to-zero), SCALE-1 (unbounded SELECT), plus 3AM-2 caught in lenses
Each PC probe shows the actual SQL or code path inspected with code blocks, making the methodology auditable
All 5 adversarial lenses applied with named sub-findings and explicit bug/improvement classification per finding
Recycle loop re-entered correctly from lens phase with explicit rationale for re-entry (not a regression)
Mechanical checks re-run post-fix (iteration 4) to confirm fixes did not break M6 tenant isolation
Failure tracker includes probe and lens failure rows alongside mechanical check rows
Improvements separated into a dedicated table (IMP-1 through IMP-5) with lens attribution
Final verdict is quantitative: 7/7 mechanical, 6 PCs probed, 3 bugs from lenses, 4 total recycled, 3 new PCs, 5 improvements, 35 passing tests
Bug descriptions cite specific function names, code patterns, and behavioural consequences (e.g., Number(null) === 0)
weaknesses: Report is long and dense — harder to scan quickly than Output A

PC-8 (pagination) is a scope expansion beyond the original contract; the forge correctly surfaces it but the decision to add it as a required postcondition rather than a non-blocking improvement may be debatable for a read-path-only fix

details.text: Contract probing covers PCs with probe angles
At least 1 bug found and recycled
